<function=python>
<parameter=code>
seg = current_frame.segmentation

        print(f"  id={n['id']} color={n['color']} hash={n['hash']} boundary={n['boundary']} children={n['children']}")

lines = current_frame.ascii.split('\n')
for i in range(26, 38):
    print(f"{i:2d}: {lines[i][8:55]}")
</parameter>
</function>
</tool_call>

sections.content:
Now the green frame with white center (id=10) is at rows 36-38, cols 12-14. The dark frame (id=8) is at rows 32-36, cols 11-15. The green frame is below the dark frame.
Looking at the right panel:
- Dark frame (id=4) at rows 26-30, cols 44-48
- Green frame with black center (id=6) at rows 30-32, cols 42-44
On the left panel:
- Dark frame (id=8) at rows 32-36, cols 11-15
- Green frame with white center (id=10) at rows 36-38, cols 12-14
The green frame with white center is now below the dark frame on the left panel, similar to how the green frame with black center is below the dark frame on the right panel. But the positions aren't exactly mirrored:
- Right panel: dark frame rows 26-30, green frame rows 30-32 (green frame starts where dark frame ends)
- Left panel: dark frame rows 32-36, green frame rows 36-38 (green frame starts where dark frame ends)
Actually, this looks like a match! The green frame is positioned just below the dark frame in both panels. But the level isn't complete yet (score is still 0).
Wait, let me look more carefully at the horizontal positions:
- Right panel: dark frame cols 44-48, green frame cols 42-44 (green frame is to the left of dark frame, overlapping at col 44)
- Left panel: dark frame cols 11-15, green frame cols 12-14 (green frame is inside dark frame horizontally)
So the horizontal positions don't match. On the right panel, the green frame is to the left of the dark frame. On the left panel, the green frame is inside the dark frame horizontally.
Maybe I need to move the green frame to the left so it's to the left of the dark frame, like on the right panel?
Let me try moving LEFT.

sections.label: TOOL CALL: python
sections.content:
<tool_call>
<function=python>
<parameter=code>
# Move LEFT to position the green frame to the left of the dark frame, matching the right panel
action(['LEFT'])
</parameter>
</function>
</tool_call>
